result:
# Introducing Smart Drafts: never stare at a blank page again

We've all been there — you open a new doc, the cursor blinks, and suddenly every idea in your head evaporates. Blank pages are where momentum goes to die, and we kept hearing the same thing from our community: the hardest part of writing isn't the writing itself, it's getting started. So we built Smart Drafts to meet you at that exact moment, turning the intimidating emptiness into something you can actually push against.

Here's how it works: give your document a title, and Smart Drafts will offer a one-click outline tailored to what you're writing. It draws on your recent docs for context, so a meeting recap looks nothing like a product brief, and a weekly update picks up where last week's left off. The outline is a starting point, not a script — keep what's useful, rewrite what isn't, and delete the rest. You stay in the driver's seat; we just hand you the keys a little faster.

This is the first step in a bigger direction. Next up is **custom voice training**, which will let Smart Drafts learn the rhythms, phrasing, and structure you already use, so the outlines it suggests feel unmistakably yours rather than generically helpful. We can't wait to see what you start with it — and as always, tell us what's working and what isn't. You know where to find us.

# Introducing Smart Drafts: never stare at a blank page again

We've all been there — you open a new doc, the cursor blinks, and suddenly every idea in your head evaporates. Blank pages are where momentum goes to die, and we kept hearing the same thing from our community: the hardest part of writing isn't the writing itself, it's getting started. So we built Smart Drafts to meet you at that exact moment, turning the intimidating emptiness into something you can actually push against.

Here's how it works: give your document a title, and Smart Drafts will offer a one-click outline tailored to what you're writing. It draws on your recent docs for context, so a meeting recap looks nothing like a product brief, and a weekly update picks up where last week's left off. The outline is a starting point, not a script — keep what's useful, rewrite what isn't, and delete the rest. You stay in the driver's seat; we just hand you the keys a little faster.

This is the first step in a bigger direction. Next up is **custom voice training**, which will let Smart Drafts learn the rhythms, phrasing, and structure you already use, so the outlines it suggests feel unmistakably yours rather than generically helpful. We can't wait to see what you start with it — and as always, tell us what's working and what isn't. You know where to find us.
